Duration: 1 rdAevel Area of Effect: 2 cu. ftAevel Saving Throw: None By employing an anin!ntc mck spell, the caster causes a stone object of up to the indicated sue to move (see the 6th-level animate object spell.) The animated stone object must be separate (not a pan of a huge boulder or the like). It follows the desire of the caste-attacking, breaking objects, block- ing-while the magic lasts. It bas no intelligence or volition of its own, but it follows instructions ex- actly as spoken. Note that only one set of instruc- tions for one single action (the whole be
[PDF_PAGE 140 | score=3] Conjure Lesser Elemental 3 and less tractable, Spells such as exaction and wrhbinding might be useful. Notes: Common for elemental specialty priests; otherwise rare. Conjure Greater Elemental (ConjnratiodSnmmoning) Reversible Sphere: Elemental Level 7 Range: 30 yds Components: V, S Casting Time: 1 turn Duration: 1 tumnevel Area of Effect: Special Saving Throw: None This spcll allows the caster to summon a single greater elemental from, in the case of priests, theit sphere of major access, or, in the case of wizards and templars. from any plane. The Hit Dice of the elemental are determined randomly. DlOO Roll Hit Dice of Elemental 01-65 10 6690 14 9140 18 This spell is otherwise identical to 
[PDF_PAGE 29 | score=2] Animal Eyes f lage, or other settlement, the beast parts company with the shukenja. In addition, misoeahhg the beast causes it to leave or even turn on the shukenja The spell does not provide telepathic M perfect communication with the beast; however, it does un- derstand the general meaning of spoken commands and gesblres. The animal is still a wild beast and can be dangemus, espxially misinterpreting common sit- uations as threatening. If well heated the animal de- fends and serves the shukenja to the best of its ability and nature. Thus, a monkey would prefer harassment and tricks to outright combaL while a tiger would fearlessly spring into the midst of the shukenja’s foes. Common animal
[PDF_PAGE 36 | score=2] Animate Object dragon’s breath or ajireball, the priest must botb beat his opponent’s initiative in the round of cast- ing and successfully take control of the flame. Under the priest’s direction, an animated flame can move at a rate of 12, although it cannot cross water or wet or muddy ground. If the priest directs the flame to leave the spell’s range, the spell ends and the flame stops, burning whatever it may be resting on. Animated flame attacks with a THACO of 10 and may he able to strike several creatures in the same round, depending on its size (see table). Creatures within the fire’s diameter are hit without an attack roll for the listed damage. Very hot or unusually cold fires may i
[PDF_PAGE 39 | score=2] Anti-Mineral Shell (Abjuration) Sphere: Protection, Elemental Earth Level: 7 Range: 0 Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 1 rd. Duration: 1 tudevel Area of Effect: 10-ft. radius Saving Throw: None The anti-ntineml shell spell creates an invisible, mobile harrier that keeps out living or animated mineral creatures and animated mineral objects. It is effective against elementals and creatures of ele- mental origin, such as crysmals and certain mephits; consmcts, such as golems and living stat- ues; creatures of living stone, such as galeh duhr or xom; and mineral objects, weapons, or mor ani- mated by some outside force Any attempt to force the harrier against such creatures or objects shatters 
[PDF_PAGE 87 | score=2] / Breath of the Elements - when the spell is cast, the priest exhales a sweet- smelling breath. lhis forms into a breeze that radiates outward, forming a circle that expands in a 50-yard radius per hour. During this time, the caster must remain at the center of the area of effect. For exam- ple, after 12 hours, the breath of life would cover a circle 1,203 yards in diameter (6oo-yard radius). The breath is of a magical nature rather than a physical nature therefore, it is unaffected by prevailing winds. The breeze blows through the community, in- stantly eliminating the specified disease from all afflicted citizens. The breath of life spell does not destmy parasitic monsters (such as green s
[PDF_PAGE 104 | score=2] such a case, the earlier dedication can be overrid- den by a new dedication performed by a priest of a higher level than the one who performed the previ- ous dedication. Investiture must be performed on any aspiring priest before that individual can achieve the status of a 1st-level priest. Consecrate item must be performed on any object to be placed on an altar or in some other location within a religious edifice. Holy (or unholy) water must be kept in a properly consecrated container, to prevent it from losing its potency. Ordination must be performed on a priest before the individual can assume responsibility for a con- gregation or assume similar duties, and even an ad- venturing priest 
[PDF_PAGE 121 | score=2] - Claws of Thad Hw- Dwarf Claws of Thard Harr - Dwarf (Alteration) Sphere: Combat Level: 1 Range: Touch Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 4 Duration: 3 ids. + 1 rd.ilevel Area of Effect: 1 dwarf Saving Throw: None This spell transforms the hands of a willing dwarf into rending talons know as the claws of Thard Ham. Each talon inflicts ld4+2 points of damage with a successful attack. Two anacks are possible per round, one with each claw. The long claws prevent the dwarf from wielding a weapon for the duration of the spell. The spell requires the priest's holy symbol and. a preserved talon of a wild beast personally slain by the caster, Neither is destroyed by the casting. Notes: Granted by th
